author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
The Apostle Peter Told No Lie, When He Said He Was Ready to Lay Down His Life for the Lord, But Only Was Ignorant of His Will.
title: The Apostle Peter Told No Lie, When He Said He Was Ready to Lay Down His Life for the Lord, But Only Was Ignorant of His Will.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: But perhaps you despise me for confessing all this, and will in consequence compare me with “cattle.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: For myself, however, I will not cease to advise you, or (if you refuse to listen to me) at all events to warn you, to acknowledge rather this common infirmity, in which virtue is perfected; lest, by assuming unknown things to be known, you fail to attain to the truth.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: For I suppose that there is something which even you wish to understand, but are unable; which you would never seek to understand, unless you hoped some day to succeed in your research.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Thus you also are ignorant of the powers of your own understanding, who profess to know all about your own nature, and decline to follow me in my confession of ignorance.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Well, there is also the will; what am I to say about that, where certainly free choice is ostentatiously claimed by us?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: The blessed Apostle Peter, indeed, was willing to lay down his life for the Lord.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: He was no doubt sincere in his willingness; nor was he treacherous to the Lord when he made the promise.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: But his will was entirely ignorant of its own powers.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Therefore the great apostle, who had discovered his Master to be the Son of God, was unknown to himself.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Thus we are quite aware respecting ourselves that we will a thing, or “nill” it; but although our will is a good one, we are ignorant, my dear son, unless we deceive ourselves, of its strength, of its resources, of what temptations it may yield to, or of what it may resist.
